Both lift stages slide prismatically within their outer leg columns as one synchronous height system, and the up and down paddles rotate on short local pivots in the controller body. Office desk scale.

A survey mast with rotary top plate. A grounded outer mast carries nested lift tubes, and the top tube carries a shallow pan head with a square face. The lift tubes are serial prismatic joints, and the pan head is a revolute joint about the vertical mast axis.

category_title: Wall Safe With Hinged Door And Dial
prompt: A recessed home wall safe with a square body, a thick right-hinged door, a round combination dial, and a short three-spoke handle below it, plus a small deposit flap above the handle. The flap breaks the door face as a separate panel rather than a stamped seam. The safe door rotates on a vertical hinge at the frame edge, the combination dial rotates continuously about its center shaft, the handle rotates on its hub, and the deposit flap rotates on a top horizontal hinge in the door face. Wall-safe scale.

A pedestal writing desk with a wide rectangular top, three left-pedestal drawers on prismatic guide rails stacked vertically with recessed oval pull handles, and three right-pedestal drawers of graduated depth each on prismatic guide rails.

A range cooker with a rotisserie: a wide stainless-steel freestanding body, a glass-panel door that drops on a full-width bottom revolute hinge with a bar pull handle, and an internal rotisserie spit rod that rotates on a revolute joint spanning the oven chamber at mid-height.

A glass observation tower elevator with a curved three-sided glass car on a vertical prismatic joint inside a round glass-encased column, and two bi-parting sliding door panels each on a horizontal prismatic joint at the car front opening.

An exposed spur reduction assembly with a handwheel input, three parallel shafts, and a plate frame that keeps every gear visible from the side. The gear diameters should vary enough to make the ratio changes obvious, and each shaft should sit in clear bearing supports. The input, idler, and output shafts are separate revolute joints about their supported axes with the spur gears meshed in one plane.

A trunnion table module. A round base stage carries two side cheeks, and a circular workplate sits between them on a transverse shaft. The base is a revolute table about the vertical axis and the workplate is a second revolute joint about the horizontal trunnion axis.

A home-care overbed table with an offset pedestal base, a rectangular laminate top, a height collar, and twin front casters tucked under the patient side, plus a smaller reading wing beside the main top. the vertical post rises from one side of the base so the top can reach over a bed or recliner, and the secondary wing is mounted on a short support bracket at the tray edge. The post slides prismatically for height adjustment, the collar knob rotates continuously on its threaded hub, and the casters rotate continuously on their wheel axles, and the reading wing rotates on its own short hinge beside the main top. A waterfront observation wheel with a hub-and-spoke wheel and fully enclosed capsules. The main wheel rotates continuously about the horizontal axle, and each capsule rotates on its yoke pivot to remain upright. Keep each capsule clipped to its yoke pivot at the rim so it stays supported during wheel rotation.

A fold-out drying rack with side wings. A central rack frame carries two wing frames and a lower support frame with hanging rails across the spans. Joints: each wing frame is revolute about its outer hinge line, and the lower support frame folds on its own revolute hinges. Favor thick rails, broad feet, and a chunky top bracket.

A nested rectangular telescoping boom. A grounded root support carries an outer box section, a middle section, and smaller inner sections that remain visibly nested. Each nested section slides on a prismatic joint along the common boom axis.

Reduce part count, simplify molds/extrusions/stampings, and keep assembly order obvious with realistic snap/bolt/clamp interfaces. Door hinges and control shafts should be obvious and manufacturable. Every piece must have a believable attachment path; no floating or orphaned components. No floating parts.

A retro two-door refrigerator with a small upper freezer door, a larger lower fresh-food door, and a rotary temperature dial inside the top trim. A single-leaf drawbridge. A fixed shore frame carries one bridge leaf on heavy side bearings. Joints: the bridge leaf is a single revolute panel about a horizontal shore-side axis. Keep the body shallow and compact around the opening.

A warehouse platform cart with a flat deck and four caster wheels. The low rectangular deck sits above corner caster mounts, and a push handle rises from one short end. Each caster wheel rotates on its axle, and each caster fork swivels on a vertical revolute pivot under the deck.

Build a precision professional Screwcap bottle tuned for calibration and repeatable alignment. A frameless all-glass three-wing revolving door for a boutique storefront. A slim central steel post carries three full-height glass panel wings. The wing assembly rotates continuously about the vertical post axis.

A tilt-and-slide sunroof cassette with a rectangular frame carrying two parallel guide rails, a glass panel that first tilts at the rear edge on a pair of pivot arms and then slides backward on the rails via rolling guide shoes at each panel corner, and a center drive cable housing connecting the panel to the mechanism. A squat pier lighthouse with a broad base, a wraparound gallery, and a cylindrical lantern housing. A plantation shutter panel with a rectangular frame and a full-height tilt rod. category_title: Sluice gate with vertical lift panel

A two-link folding linkage. A root clevis holds a short primary link and a longer secondary link ending in a rectangular tab. The shoulder and elbow are revolute joints about parallel axes in one plane, with enough range for the linkage to fold close to the base and extend nearly straight.

A brushed-metal zippo-style lighter with a boxy lower shell, an upper lid, and a visible chimney insert inside the case, plus a lift-out insert fit inside the lower shell. the insert remains a separate supported part seated inside the outer shell, and the insert remains visibly removable from the outer case. A Newton's cradle desk toy with a rectangular metal frame, two horizontal top rails, and five steel ball pendulums. Each ball hangs from a rigid V-shaped rod pair that pivots on a revolute joint at the top rail. Compact executive-desk scale.

A multi-segment fold-out arm. A fixed base plate carries four short boxed links in series ending at a small platform bracket. The four revolute joints fold in one plane so the arm packs near the base and extends outward into a straight or slightly arched reach.

A compact three-joint robot arm. A short base column supports a large shoulder link, a slimmer forearm, and a wrist yoke with a square output plate. The arm uses three serial revolute joints: shoulder at the base, elbow between the links, and wrist at the end of the forearm.

A DJ turntable with a rectangular plinth base, a flat circular platter on a central continuous revolute axle, a pivoting tonearm on a vertical revolute pivot joint at the arm base, a horizontal arm-height anti-skate revolute at the pivot, and a cueing lever on a revolute hinge at the plinth front edge.

A marine porro-prism binocular with wide sealed objective barrels in a rubber-armored housing, two barrels on a central revolute hinge, a center focus wheel on a revolute joint, and a built-in compass housing that rotates on a revolute joint set into the right barrel body.

An independent rear trailing-arm suspension with a flat transverse cross-member, two trailing arms each on a horizontal revolute mount joint at the cross-member ends, and a wheel hub on a revolute axle at each trailing arm tip.

A low-bypass military turbojet with a long cylindrical core and a pointed intake cone. A playground glider swing with a two-seat bench suspended by rigid side links from an overhead frame. A searchlight tower with a pan-tilt lamp head. A mast support carries a yoke, and the lamp body sits between the yoke arms. Joints: the head pans on a vertical revolute base and tilts on a horizontal revolute yoke axis. Make the rotating member large relative to the fixed support.

A wall-mounted quarter-candy dispenser with a rectangular clear hopper, a metal front plate, and a lower retrieval flap. A rectangular monitor-stand lazy Susan with a broad top platform over a round base. The boxy top plate is centered on a low turntable ring and a circular bottom disc. The top platform rotates continuously about the vertical revolute axis at the center bearing.

Push the realism with machined guide interfaces, bracketed cable channels, end buffers, and believable beam and leg proportions. The beam rider is one horizontal prismatic joint and the nested Z carriage is one vertical prismatic joint. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.
A long refractor telescope on a cast-iron alt-az mount with a fine-focus knob on a revolute joint and a three-leg tripod.

A fixie single-speed crankset with a press-fit bottom-bracket shell, two forged alloy crank arms on a revolute spindle, a single narrow track chainring bolted to the right crank, and flat platform pedals spinning on stub axle revolute joints.

A compact hobby drill press with a slim column, a square tilting table, and a small rack-height crank at the table bracket. The table slides prismatically on the column, the table rotates on a horizontal hinge at its support bracket, and the height crank rotates continuously on its short shaft. Small workshop scale.

A stair-climbing hand truck with tri-wheel clusters on both sides. The rigid frame carries one three-wheel bogie at each side near the base, and a narrow toe plate projects from the front edge. Each small wheel spins on its axle, and each three-wheel bogie rotates about its central hub relative to the frame.
A fat bike fork with a wide alloy crown, oversized blades, a steerer on a revolute joint in a tapered head tube, and wide flat handlebars.

A two-axis instrument head with a pedestal, an outer roll ring, and an inner pitch cradle carrying a small tool plate. The nested supports should feel compact and balanced rather than turret-like, with the two axis packages clearly distinguishable. Use realistic detail such as machined ring faces, trunnion caps, small service covers, and believable wall thickness where the two axes nest together. The outer ring is a revolute roll axis about a front-to-back shaft, and the inner cradle is a revolute pitch axis about a left-to-right shaft. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A slim utility knife with a top thumb slider and a side safety latch. The rigid handle shell surrounds the blade track and carries a small latch tab in the side wall. The blade carriage slides prismatically along the nose-to-tail axis, and the safety latch rotates on its own small pivot in the handle side.

A deluxe food processor with a broad handled bowl, a lift-up lid, two side latch arms, and a front control band with a selector dial plus two rocker buttons. The lid rotates upward on a rear hinge, the feed pusher slides prismatically in the main chute, the latch arms rotate on matched pivots, and the cutting blade rotates continuously on the center spindle. The selector dial rotates continuously, and both rocker buttons pivot independently on short axes. The bowl and chute should read as clear hollow shells with a real prep cavity around the blade. Large countertop scale.

A compact rental knee scooter with a simple front fork and a rear brake bar. The center frame carries four wheels and a padded knee deck, and a small brake bar pivots above the rear axle. The front fork rotates on a vertical steering axis, the wheels spin continuously on their axles, and the brake bar rotates on a small rear pivot above the back wheels.

A low wide-profile metronome with a nearly flat tapered housing and a wide horizontal pendulum slot across the front face. A pendulum rod with a flat rectangular sliding weight swings through the slot from an internal center pivot; a winding key sits flush in the top face. The pendulum rotates on a revolute joint at the internal center shaft; the weight slides prismatically along the rod; the key turns continuously about the top-face axis.

A robotic leg module for a walking machine. An upper leg housing carries a thigh link, shank link, and compact ankle-foot section. Joints: hip revolute about a lateral axis, knee revolute about a lateral axis, ankle revolute about a lateral axis. Use a centered link layout with the moving chain close to the midline.

A compact service three-axis wrist. A broad grounded body supports a lower rotary stage, a pitch cradle, and a compact roll flange with clear spacing between each stage. The stage sequence is yaw revolute, pitch revolute, then roll revolute.

A chimney-style kitchen range hood with a rectangular canopy and a tall chimney chase. One large central rotary knob is flanked by two small push-buttons on the left and two on the right in a symmetric five-control layout. Only these controls articulate, with the knob rotating continuously and the four buttons moving inward prismatically.

A wall-mounted compact washer with a slim white housing on a wall bracket, a drum on a revolute axle, and a round porthole door with a rubber gasket ring hinged on the left edge on two barrel revolute hinges.

A sightseeing blimp with a streamlined main envelope, a long passenger cabin, and one propeller pod mounted on each side near the cabin, plus vectoring engine pylons beside the gondola. the tail keeps a real cruciform fin arrangement at the rear of the hull, and the engine pods stay visibly carried by those pylons. The two propellers spin continuously on their shaft axes, the rudder pivots on a vertical tail hinge, and the elevators pivot on horizontal hinges at the tailplane roots, and the engine pods rotate on short pivots at the pylon ends. Passenger airship scale.

A stackable rolling toolbox base with a large lower bin, a telescoping handle, and two side latches securing the lid, plus a shallow front drawer with a finger pull. the lid and body keep clear part breaks suitable for a rugged polymer tool box, and the drawer remains aligned within the lower shell. The handle slides prismatically on twin rear rails, the lid rotates on a rear hinge, the two side latches rotate on their own pivots, and the wheels spin continuously on their axle, and the drawer slides prismatically on straight guide runners. Workshop storage scale.

An undershot waterwheel. Two side frames support a horizontal axle carrying a paddle wheel below a simple channel or trough edge. Joints: the wheel rotates continuously about the axle axis. Keep the rotating member centered on the main axis of the support.

A desk fan with a tilting head. A base supports a guarded fan head between two side brackets. Joints: the fan rotor spins continuously about its axle, and the head tilts on a horizontal revolute axis through the side brackets. Make the rotating member small relative to a broader fixed support.

An under-shelf monitor mount shown without a screen, with a top plate, a folding two-link arm, and a compact rectangular head frame that can tuck close to the plate. The geometry should feel purpose-built for folding into a shallow envelope. The two arm joints are revolute folds on supported pivots, and the head has an independent pan joint and a pitch hinge.

A rack-style router with a pair of folding handles and a front power switch. The shallow rack body has one hinged handle at each front corner and a small rocker switch near the center. The two handles rotate on their side pivots at the rack ears, and the rocker switch rotates on a short transverse pivot in the front panel.

A utility pickup tailgate with a simple drop panel and a center release button. The gate spans the rear opening on two lower hinges and carries a push-release button near the top center. The tailgate rotates on the horizontal lower hinge axis, and the release button moves prismatically into the latch housing.

A streaming remote with a narrow body, a circular navigation pad, a side volume rocker, and a sliding battery door on the back shell. The side volume rocker pivots on a short local hinge, the visible front buttons depress independently as small prismatic controls, and the battery door slides prismatically along the back of the housing. Living-room remote scale.

A realistic, highly detailed air purifier with a clean appliance body, venting surfaces, and a believable filter access door. The filter door and standard controls articulate naturally, and the object should feel like a polished home appliance with realistic paneling and material transitions.

An H-frame easel with a broad base, a telescoping center mast, and a pivoting canvas holder arm at the top. The center mast slides prismatically within the frame, and the top canvas holder arm rotates on a horizontal hinge at the mast head. Studio display scale.

A bakery-corner stand mixer with a thick painted pedestal, a bowl on a short front carriage, and a substantial head with a wire whisk. Make the kinematic structure explicit: the bowl translates prismatically relative to the base, the head tilts on one rear horizontal revolute hinge, the whisk rotates continuously beneath the head, the speed control on the base rotates on a small revolute joint, and the head-lock control on the base translates on a short prismatic stroke.

A three-joint robotic arm for pick-and-place work. A pedestal base carries a shoulder housing, upper arm link, forearm link, and compact wrist head. Joints: shoulder revolute about vertical, elbow revolute about horizontal, wrist revolute about the forearm axis. Spread the joints apart so each axis is easy to read in profile.

A compact countertop juicer with a rounded base, a hinged top lid, and a vertical feed pusher above the juicing basket. A short anti-drip spout projects from the front of the chamber, and a small speed lever sits on the side of the base. The feed pusher slides prismatically in the chute, the lid rotates on a rear hinge, the anti-drip spout rotates on a small front pivot, the speed lever rotates on a short side pivot, and the juicing basket rotates continuously about the vertical axis.

A coastal-defense launcher with a rotating pedestal and an elevating enclosed pod. The heavy base supports a yaw ring, and the enclosed launch pod is carried between side cheeks on a trunnion axis. The base rotates on a vertical revolute axis, and the pod rotates on a horizontal revolute axis through the side cheeks.

A home workshop floor pump with a wide base, a vertical barrel, a top T-handle, and a short hose wrap hook on the frame, plus a folding handle grip around the top bar. the frame keeps the barrel upright with simple stamped or tubular supports, and the grip remains a supported sleeve on the crossbar. The T-handle and piston rod slide prismatically on the barrel axis and the pressure gauge needle rotates on its central axle, and the grip rotates continuously about the crossbar axis. Household workshop scale.
